Patch Set 36:

Ok, after analysis, I have been able to identify that the recent auto-tests failures/TIMEOUTs in sanity-hsm/test_13 for this patch were due to a wrong rebase where a call to mdt_cdt_remove_request() has been removed from mdt_hsm_update_request_state() causing HSM requests to stay present in memory and thus cause max_requests to be reached preventing new requests start!
Secondly, I have also found that the previous auto-tests errors for this patch in both sanity-hsm/test_220a and sanity-hsm/test_222c were due to regression introduced by the new CDT behavior with this patch, now preventing immediate handling of the ARCHIVE/RESTORE request and allowing for the "rm -f" effect to be triggered in mdt_hsm_get_md_hsm() and then, since change “LU-9312 hsm: fix error handling around mdt_hsm_get_md_hsm()“ is presently missing, the request to be repeatedly returned to its WAITING state!! 
I will push a new/updated version for these 2 problems, to reinstate mdt_cdt_remove_request() call in mdt_hsm_update_request_state() and to add a “wait_request_state $fid [ARCHIVE|RESTORE] STARTED“ statement between action launch and "rm -f" in order to ensure the request to reach CT before the file unlink.
Last, seems that there are cases, in sanity-hsm/test_[220a,222c,...] treating ChangeLog accurate error logging cases, where the ChangeLog flag can wrongly contain DIRTY flag, my guess is that it is because patch from LU-9312 already noticed before is missing the PTR_ERR(obj) case during HS_DIRTY check in hsm_cdt_request_completed(), because in this case "struct md_hsm mh" contain can not be used since it has not been filled in/by mdt_hsm_get_md_hsm() !!! I will create a separate ticket/fix for this special case.

commit.subject: LU-7988 hsm: run HSM coordinator once per second at most
commit.message:
LU-7988 hsm: run HSM coordinator once per second at most

When there is heavy HSM usage, each new HSM request can trigger the
HSM coordinator, which may run many times per seconds. When it is
running it locks the HSM catalog (using cdt_llog_lock) preventing any
other HSM operation to happen, such as insertion, removal or dumping
of the requests.

Limit the coordinator to run once per second, and only if there is
work to do. It will still execute the loop once every 10 seconds (or
as defined by the procfs loop_period parameter) to do housekeeping.
